name: MaintenanceDeluxe
overview: Premium maintenance overlay with live countdown and rich release notes.
description: Premium cinema-themed maintenance overlay for Jellyfin. Full-screen velours-and-gold overlay with a drifting aurora background, a rotating film-reel logo, crackling ember particles and a brushed-metal card border. Live countdown, time-based progress bar, admin-configurable release notes with quick-add templates and emoji palette. Overlay suppressed on admin pages so admins always have access.

versions.changelog: v0.3.2 - Power-user features + filet de securite tests. (1) Export / Import config JSON : bouton Exporter dans le tab Maintenance qui telecharge la config en JSON (URL webhook strippee par <redacted>). Bouton Importer ouvre une preview modal avec validation (refus si webhook URL pas redacted). L'import touche que le formulaire, le serveur reste intact tant que tu n'as pas clique Enregistrer. (2) Uptime Kuma helper : bouton 'Construire depuis Uptime Kuma' a cote du champ URL de statut. Modal qui demande l'URL de l'instance + slug, genere l'URL finale et permet de la tester via HEAD no-cors. (3) Validation contraste WCAG : warning soft sous le picker de couleur d'accent si le ratio avec le fond est < 4.5:1 (norme AA). Calcul de luminance relative selon la formule officielle WCAG 2.1. (4) Projet de tests xUnit : 61 tests unitaires sur les fonctions pures (DetectFormat, NormaliseHexColor, NormaliseTheme, NormaliseAnimationSpeed/ParticleDensity/BorderStyle, NormaliseReleaseNotes, IsUrlSafe). Etape dotnet test ajoutee au workflow CI - aucune PR ne pourra plus casser une normalisation sans s'en rendre compte. (5) CHANGELOG.md au format Keep a Changelog avec backfill complet depuis v0.2.0.

v0.3.1.1 - Patch CSS sur la liste blanche : les checkboxes natives Jellyfin (is='emby-checkbox') chevauchaient les noms d'utilisateurs en mode flex. Le widget passe en grid 2 colonnes (checkbox 1.6em fixe + label flex), checkboxes HTML natives avec sizing explicite, plus de chevauchement possible quel que soit le nombre d'utilisateurs.

v0.3.1 - Webhooks Discord/Slack + whitelist utilisateurs. (1) Notifications webhook : envoie un payload HTTP a chaque activation/desactivation de maintenance. Format Discord, Slack ou JSON generique auto-detecte depuis l'URL. Bouton 'Tester la notification' avec rate-limit 1 appel/5s par IP. URL HTTPS uniquement (pas de http://). Si le webhook est down, la maintenance s'active/se desactive quand meme - le webhook ne peut jamais bloquer le toggle. Doc payloads : docs/webhooks.md. (2) Liste blanche utilisateurs : nouveau widget multi-select dans la section Maintenance qui liste tous les utilisateurs Jellyfin. Les utilisateurs coches conservent leur acces pendant la maintenance (parfait pour la famille, l'astreinte, un compte de test). Les utilisateurs supprimes apparaissent en italique avec un bouton 'Retirer'. Edge case : un user ajoute apres l'activation reste desactive jusqu'au prochain toggle. (3) Privacy : l'endpoint public /MaintenanceDeluxe/maintenance ne renvoie plus les listes UUID (whitelist, disabled, pre-disabled) ni l'URL webhook - elles ne fuitent plus aux callers anonymes. Le admin UI passe par /config (auth) qui contient le state complet. (4) Backend : nouveau WebhookNotifier statique avec timeout 5s + 1 retry. Endpoint POST /maintenance/test-webhook et GET /users-summary, tous deux RequiresElevation.

v0.3.0 - Quality release: bug fixes + anti-data-loss + French admin UI. (1) Bugs: BannerController endpoints now return 503 if the plugin instance is not yet initialised instead of serving phantom defaults; ScheduledRestart is preserved when set after a maintenance window (only cleared if it was inside the window); MutationObserver and hideScrollObserver are now properly torn down on pagehide (no more memory leak after long sessions); live-preview postMessage receiver enforces same-origin checks (no cross-origin injection of overlay config). (2) Anti-data-loss: orange dot badge next to Save when there are unsaved changes, browser warning if you try to close the tab with unsaved changes, automatic localStorage draft autosaved every 500ms with restore prompt on next load, inline validation that flags scheduledEnd before scheduledStart and scheduledRestart in the past. (3) French admin UI: the Maintenance tab, Save button, save-confirmation modal, alerts and validations are fully translated to French. (4) The live-preview iframe now shows a clear fallback after a 5s timeout if the iframe never reports ready.

v0.2.3 - Two polish fixes on the new Appearance panel. First, the 'Agrandir l'apercu' button used Jellyfin's default emby-button which rendered with a white background on the dark admin theme; it now uses the same neutral-translucent style as the Reset button so it fits both light and dark themes. Second, new 'Rotatif' border option: a luminous gold gradient that slowly spins around the card, paired with a soft pulsing glow. Speed follows the animation scale slider (including the 'off' toggle).

v0.2.2 - Fine-tune beyond the presets. Every Animation speed and Particle density preset now has a companion numeric input (0-5× scale for animations, 0-500 for particle count). Click a preset to fill the number; type a number to override with any value in-range. Layout: appearance preview is now sticky (it follows you as you scroll the controls column), iframe min-height bumped to 540px so long release notes fit, controls column is tighter, expand-mode settings panel narrowed from 340px to 300px so the preview gets more room.

v0.2.1 - Polish on the new Appearance section: live preview iframe now loads correctly (the preview shell and banner.js endpoints no longer require Authorization headers that browsers don't send on iframe navigation, fixes 401 errors). The three enum selectors (animation speed, particle density, card border) are now segmented pill controls instead of plain radio buttons - much more readable and consistent with modern UI. Reset appearance button restyled so it is legible on both light and dark themes. Appearance labels, alerts and preview copy translated to French.

v0.2.0 - Appearance customization with live preview. New admin section lets you pick accent color, background tint, card opacity, animation speed (off/slow/normal/fast), particle density (none/low/normal/dense), and card border style (full gradient / simple gold / none). A live preview iframe reflects every change instantly without saving. Expand button gives a fullscreen view with toggleable settings panel (H hotkey) and Esc to exit. All colors are now derived from a single accent pick, so the palette stays coherent whatever hue you choose.

v0.1.13 - UI FIX: gold card border was truncated when release notes made the overlay taller than the viewport. The card had its own internal scroll (max-height:92vh) so the ::before gold gradient didn't extend through scrolled content. Fix: overlay scrolls instead of card, card grows to full content height so the border wraps everything, and background layers (blobs, beams, grain, particles) are pinned to viewport with position:fixed.

v0.1.12 - CRITICAL FIX: Save button stopped reacting as soon as any field was modified. Root cause: a TypeError in collectMessages() aborted initDom before the Save handler got wired, because release-note rows shared the .jf-msg-row class with rotation messages and had no .jf-msg-detail. Fix: excluded .jf-rn-row from the rotation collector + defensive null guard in collectRowData.

v0.1.11 - CRITICAL FIX: admin config page was entirely unresponsive since v0.1.8. Root cause: release-note templates had real newlines inside JS string literals, causing the whole inline admin script to fail parsing. Every button, input, and save handler silently died. Now fixed — admin UI fully interactive again, tested with node --check.
